description.value:
Baseline (0 Spirituality): Spend 1 action analyzing one scene object, spoken statement, sigil, coded note, or behavior pattern within 60 feet. Make a Spirituality (Investigation) check against DC 13 (or opposed by active deception). On success, extract one hidden structure: true intent, missing clause, disguised trigger, or concealed linkage between two clues.

Cryptologist Limit: You reveal structure, not omniscience. If the clue was never present, Decryption cannot fabricate it.

Higher Spend (upcast):

  • +1 Spirituality: Ask one additional focused question about the same target and get one extra reliable clue.
  • +2 Spirituality: Extend to all relevant clues in a 20-foot area and identify one deliberate false lead automatically.
  • +4 Spirituality: Bind one decoded weakness for 10 minutes; first ally check exploiting that weakness gains advantage and +Potency bonus.

Counterplay: Layered ciphers, anti-divination seals, fabricated memories, or intentionally contradictory evidence can impose disadvantage or partial reads.

Corruption Hook: If you knowingly conceal a decrypted lethal risk from allies for personal gain, gain 1 Corruption.

Legacy Upgrade (Sequence 5 - Potency)

At Sequence 5, Decryption can parse bizarro-level contradiction seams. If Decryption is cast with at least +2 Spirituality, you may designate one decoded falsehood as "compromised" for 1 minute: the first creature relying on that falsehood takes -1d4 on its related check or save, and your next Clause Hijack against it gains advantage.

Legacy Upgrade (Sequence 4 - Efficiency)

At Sequence 4, Decryption can parse host-state feeds from active parasites. When targeting a parasitized host, Decryption may be used as a bonus action and gains a minimum result floor equal to 10 + Potency for non-opposed checks. If cast with at least +2 Spirituality, you may immediately chain one free yes/no clarification about current host intent.

Legacy Upgrade (Sequence 3 - Scope)

At Sequence 3, Decryption may treat one active Deceit construct as a readable object. When this interaction is used, Decryption can map up to three linked contradiction seams at once, and one ally of your choice gains advantage on the first check exploiting any mapped seam this round.
